items.name: everything in one platform
items.content: everything in one platform and keep an accurate inventory
items.quote: everything in one platform and keep an accurate inventory
items.description: Buyers frame the win as consolidation+accuracy across decades, not features. AA already owns all-in-one inventory, so Artelier must differentiate on what AA lacks: deterministic pricing and the sale workflow.
items.source: Artwork Archive review; user cataloguing 20+ years of work

items.name: affirmed my sense of myself as a professional artist
items.content: affirmed my sense of myself as a professional artist
items.quote: affirmed my sense of myself as a professional artist
items.description: The product sells identity before utility. Lead onboarding and pricing copy with professional legitimacy; frame the engine as 'price like a pro,' not 'calculate faster.'
items.source: Artwork Archive review; identity-first adoption

items.name: the gallerist wished every artist were as organized
items.content: the gallerist wished every artist they worked with were as organized
items.quote: the gallerist wished every artist they worked with were as organized
items.description: Validation artists crave is external, from gatekeepers. Design the One Sheet to impress the person on the other side of the table, not just to store data.
items.source: Artwork Archive review; external gatekeeper validation

items.name: more information about how to handle consignments
items.content: would like more information about how to handle consignments
items.quote: would like more information about how to handle consignments
items.description: Even satisfied incumbent users hit a wall on consignment/exhibitions, the exact multi-party money logic Artelier's Settlement waterfall handles. A namable wedge.
items.source: Artwork Archive review; consignment workflow gap

items.name: distinguish historical artists from current contacts
items.content: not an easy way to distinguish historical artists from current contacts
items.quote: not an easy way to distinguish historical artists from current contacts
items.description: Roster vs contact separation is a known incumbent weakness. Artelier's per-artist seat model makes this structural rather than a tagging hack, which matters for the manager ICP.
items.source: Artwork Archive review; roster/contact modeling weakness

items.name: spreadsheets quickly become cumbersome
items.content: spreadsheets quickly becomes cumbersome and requires quite a bit of manual updating
items.quote: spreadsheets quickly becomes cumbersome and requires quite a bit of manual updating
items.description: The true competitor is the spreadsheet; its failure mode is manual updating plus labeling plus certificates. Target the breaking moment in top-of-funnel copy.
items.source: Independent review of inventory tooling; the spreadsheet failure mode

items.name: my spreadsheet just doesnt update itself
items.content: My spreadsheet just doesn't update itself
items.quote: My spreadsheet just doesn't update itself
items.description: Raw emotional language: exhaustion, drowning, wants something simple. Empathy-first hook will beat feature copy at top of funnel.
items.source: Etsy seller forum; raw overwhelm

items.name: many do not own their data they offload to the gallery
items.content: many do not own their data once they establish a relationship with a gallery they offload the cataloguing and tracking to that gallery
items.quote: many do not own their data once they establish a relationship with a gallery they offload the cataloguing and tracking to that gallery
items.description: Signing with a gallery means losing ownership of your provenance/sales record. 'Own your data, own your career' is a wedge that resonates hardest with the growing gallery-light segment.
items.source: Valise/Sholis interview; own-your-data thesis

items.name: most asked question among young artists is how to price
items.content: the most asked question online among young artists is how to price my original art
items.quote: the most asked question online among young artists is how to price my original art
items.description: Direct third-party validation of the pricing-engine moat. The market repeatedly asks the exact question Artelier answers deterministically. Lead the funnel with pricing.
items.source: Artist Substack; pricing as #1 pain

items.name: art is worth what someone will pay for it
items.content: art is worth what someone will pay for it
items.quote: art is worth what someone will pay for it
items.description: The enemy is the belief that pricing is arbitrary. A deterministic engine reframes pricing from anxiety to defensible logic. Attack the arbitrariness head-on in copy.
items.source: Pricing guide; the arbitrariness belief

items.name: be prepared to explain how you determined the value
items.content: be prepared to explain how you've determined your artwork's value
items.quote: be prepared to explain how you've determined your artwork's value
items.description: Artists want defensibility in front of buyers and galleries. Surface the why behind each price (size, tier, comp) as a collector-facing rationale, a feature manual pricing cannot match.
items.source: Pricing guide; defensibility need

items.name: maintain consistent pricing across all sales channels
items.content: maintain consistent pricing across all sales channels
items.quote: maintain consistent pricing across all sales channels
items.description: Consistency is a stated professional value; a single source-of-truth engine enforces it automatically. Especially valuable for managers juggling multiple artists and channels.
items.source: Pricing guide; consistency as trust

items.name: not sure whether it is worth $29 per month
items.content: not sure whether it is worth $29 per month but I will continue to use it as it is the only AR app available
items.quote: not sure whether it is worth $29 per month but I will continue to use it as it is the only AR app available
items.description: Value doubt is explicit even among users who stay (locked in, not loyal). Artelier's trial must deliver an unmissable value moment in week one or churn risk is high.
items.source: ArtPlacer review; price-to-value doubt despite lock-in

items.name: two years and have to downgrade to 2 stars
items.content: I initially gave it 5 stars using the app for more than 2 years and have to downgrade to 2
items.quote: I initially gave it 5 stars using the app for more than 2 years and have to downgrade to 2
items.description: Long-term churn is driven by stagnation, promised features that never ship. A user-facing changelog/roadmap could be a retention feature, not just internal hygiene.
items.source: ArtPlacer review; churn from stagnation

items.name: canceled and they keep charging me
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11 from G2 Artlogic reviews.
items.description: The leader's worst failure is billing trust plus dead support. Make cancellation frictionless and self-serve and say so: 'cancel in one click, no hostage tactics.'
items.source: Artlogic G2 review; billing-trust failure

items.name: built by developers not people who run galleries
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11. Independent review of Artlogic, ARTERNAL, ArtBinder.
items.description: Heard near-verbatim from four directors; a systematic empathy gap. Artelier's wedge is workflow-native design; the 7-stage state machine mirrors how art actually changes hands.
items.source: Independent review aggregating 4 gallery directors

items.name: price increases with no feature improvements PE-owned
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11. One Berlin gallery cited ~40% increase over 18 months.
items.description: PE ownership equals an extraction narrative buyers resent. Artelier's founder ownership plus 15% community fund is the anti-extraction counter-story. This is where give-back becomes positioning.
items.source: Independent review; extraction narrative

items.name: basic reports require exporting to Excel
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11.
items.description: Even the leader dumps users back into the spreadsheet they paid to escape. Artelier's zero-export native one-sheet is a demonstrable superiority; show a side-by-side.
items.source: Independent review; incumbent pushes users back to spreadsheets

items.name: a cost that only makes sense for established artists
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11.
items.description: The leader explicitly prices out Artelier's exact ICP at £492/yr. EUR 9-39/mo is a structural price wedge, and the underserved segment is also the growing one.
items.source: Hands-on Artlogic review; prices out the solo artist

items.name: replicate gallery bureaucracy
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11.
items.description: Complexity is a feature for galleries and a bug for independents. A product-discipline warning: resist feature-creep toward gallery-grade complexity; guard onboarding simplicity.
items.source: Hands-on Artlogic review; complexity as a bug for independents

items.name: ArtPlacer architected for galleries and full-time professionals
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11.
items.description: ArtPlacer competes on visualization (AR), not business operations. Own the transaction backbone it ignores; treat it as a possible integration, not a rival.
items.source: Competitor comparison; ArtPlacer is adjacent not core

items.name: misleading offer first it says it is free
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11. Includes refund-refusal complaint on annual plan.
items.description: Trial/pricing transparency is a sore point across competitors. Artelier's card-required trial must state day-30 behavior plainly; transparency itself is a differentiator here.
items.source: ArtPlacer Shopify reviews; trial transparency complaints

items.name: Artwork Archive owns most affordable and intuitive
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11. Plans from $6/mo (artist/collector).
items.description: AA owns affordable+intuitive+all-in-one inventory at $6/mo. Artelier cannot out-cheap it on inventory; the durable wedge is the pricing engine plus sale workflow AA is silent on.
items.source: AA category positioning at $6/mo

items.name: Artwork Archive entrenchment 4.9 stars 106 reviews
items.content: Captured 2026-06-11. 4.9/5, 106 reviews, 89% small business.
items.description: AA is entrenched and loved in Artelier's exact segment. Win by category re-definition (business OS with pricing engine), not a feature war on AA's turf.
items.source: AA scale and segment dominance

items.name: global art market $59.6B in 2025 up 4%
items.content: Total global art sales rose 4% YoY to ~USD 59.6B in 2025.
items.description: TAM is large and recovering but the topline is the wrong frame for Artelier's ICP. Use for scale only; build the narrative on the segment trend.
items.source: Art Basel UBS 2026 report; topline

items.name: lower-end strong dealers under $500k double-digit growth
items.content: Double-digit increases in average sales for dealers turning over less than $500,000.
items.description: Artelier's ICP is the single growing segment while the top whipsaws. This is the core investment thesis: build rails for the expanding, ignored segment.
items.source: Art Basel UBS 2026 report; segment trend

items.name: middle market $50k-250k down 29% since 2010
items.content: Works priced $50,000-$250,000 down 29% since 2010.
items.description: The squeezed middle is hollowing out; activity moves to lower prices and more transactions. More transactions at lower values is a per-seat SaaS tailwind. Quantify in the raise.
items.source: Artsy on Art Basel UBS report; the collapsing middle

items.name: 40.5M transactions up 3% as value fell
items.content: Transaction count grew 3% to 40.5 million in 2024 as value fell 12%.
items.description: Artelier monetizes per-artist/per-workflow, not per-dollar-of-art, so it benefits from rising transaction volume regardless of art prices. Insulated from value swings.
items.source: Art Basel UBS report; volume decoupling from value

items.name: France +9% to $4.5B Switzerland +13%
items.content: France grew 9% to $4.5B; Switzerland up 13% in 2025.
items.description: France (home) and Switzerland (Legal Studio target) both grow above the global average. France-first launch is defensible on data, not just convenience.
items.source: Art Basel UBS 2026 report; home and Legal Studio markets growing

items.name: online sales $10.5B 76% above 2019 social channels opening
items.content: Online sales $10.5B in 2024, still 76% above 2019; Instagram/WeChat channels opening.
items.description: Direct/social selling is structurally elevated, validating the Facebook-DM motion and the shelved WhatsApp pipeline. Instrument the DM; don't build a marketplace.
items.source: Art Basel UBS report; direct/social selling elevated

items.name: 35 artists under contract no POS handles the split
items.content: We currently have 35 artists under contract
items.description: The manager ICP's core unmet need: roster-wide commission/consignment accounting mainstream tools ignore. Artelier's per-artist Settlement waterfall is the native answer; 'pay every artist correctly, automatically' is the manager headline.
items.source: Small gallery roster 25-30 artists; mainstream POS won't track artist commissions; cobbled together a consignment-shop tool

items.name: WHO sets the market value who is doing the pricing
items.content: WHO sets the market value of the art Who is doing the pricing
items.quote: WHO sets the market value of the art Who is doing the pricing
items.description: Pricing authority is unresolved even among gallerists, and artists want independence from gallery commissions. Pricing+independence unites the solo and manager ICPs; both point back to the engine.
items.source: Gallery owner mid-debate on commissions; artists want independence

items.name: ArtCloud built around a stable of artists
items.content: Captured 2026-06-29. Now inside the Artlogic PE roll-up.
items.description: ArtCloud (Artlogic-acquired July 2025) is the closest manager-workflow competitor and sets the feature bar: connected accounts, per-artist consignment, auto payouts. Match the workflow; differentiate on the pricing engine plus founder-owned anti-extraction story.
items.source: ArtCloud manager product: connected artist accounts, per-artist consignment %, auto-calculated payouts

items.name: multi-user team access is table stakes for managers
items.content: Captured 2026-06-29.
items.description: Granular per-seat access (manager sees roster; each artist sees own work) is expected across incumbents; table stakes not a differentiator. Artelier's per-artist seat model must match it cleanly or fail the manager's first evaluation.
items.source: Art Galleria/Managed Artwork/ArtCloud all offer invite-others and staff permissions

items.name: French pricing anxiety un veritable casse-tete
items.content: Arriver a fixer un prix juste pour son propre travail est un veritable casse-tete
items.quote: Arriver a fixer un prix juste pour son propre travail est un veritable casse-tete
items.description: French pricing anxiety is identical to anglophone, arguably more acute and emotional. The #1 category pain translates directly; lead the French funnel with the same pricing hook at the same register.
items.source: French artist content: pricing your own work is a real headache; Combien vaut mon travail

items.name: le calcul au point et la cote d'artiste
items.content: la cote d'artiste se calcule selon votre anciennete le nombre d'expositions
items.quote: la cote d'artiste se calcule selon votre anciennete le nombre d'expositions
items.description: Biggest localization insight: France already trusts a size-times-career pricing method (calcul au point, cote d'artiste) that maps 1:1 onto Artelier's engine. In France, automate the method the market already trusts; speak calcul au point and cote d'artiste natively for near-frictionless adoption.
items.source: France's native standardized size-and-career pricing method taught by galleries and cotation bodies

items.name: French objection formula pricing tres basique et illogique
items.content: je trouve votre systeme de calcul tres basique et illogique
items.quote: je trouve votre systeme de calcul tres basique et illogique
items.description: Counter-voice to pre-empt: some artists reject formula pricing as mechanical/unfair. Position the engine as a defensible floor and starting point, not a cage: le calcul fixe le plancher, votre cote et votre marche font le reste.
items.source: French reader rejects point-based pricing as ignoring craft and emotion

items.name: French e-invoicing reform dated forcing function
items.content: From 1 Sep 2026 all VAT-subject entities incl artistes-auteurs with SIRET must receive structured e-invoices via approved platform; emit from 1 Sep 2027; PDF emailed no longer valid; up to 15 EUR/invoice penalty.
items.description: The biggest French tailwind: the state forces every small VAT-subject artist/manager off spreadsheets and PDFs onto compliant tooling, on Artelier's launch timeline and aimed at its ICP. The core change-adoption friction is solved by law. Marketing hook: Septembre 2026 arrive, soyez pret.
items.source: From 1 Sep 2026 receive, 1 Sep 2027 emit structured e-invoices via approved platform; PDF no longer valid; penalty per invoice

items.name: compliance trap PDF is not a compliant e-invoice Artelier not a PDP
items.content: Structured formats Factur-X UBL CII via Plateforme Agreee required for B2B; PDF/scanned/emailed not compliant.
items.description: Opportunity and risk are the same fact. Artelier's PDFShift PDF output becomes non-compliant for French B2B emission by Sep 2027. Decide whether to integrate with/hand off to an approved PDP; this must be in the pending expert-comptable/avocat scope. Don't promise compliant French invoicing without PDP integration.
items.source: Reform requires structured Factur-X/UBL/CII through an approved platform; PDF/scanned/emailed explicitly non-compliant

items.name: French emerging-artist resource runs WhatsApp not newsletter
items.content: Pas de Newsletters ici mais une chaine d'actu WhatsApp
items.description: A French resource targeting emerging artists chooses WhatsApp over newsletter, corroborating the WhatsApp ingestion instinct for this segment. The emerging/French cohort lives on WhatsApp; reinforces the shelved WhatsApp pipeline as a French-market priority and a possible acquisition channel.
items.source: A French emerging-artist site chooses a WhatsApp channel over email
